<a name="TTLOption"></a>
## type [TTLOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/etcd/registrar.go#L36-L39>)

TTLOption allow setting a key with a TTL. This option will be used by a loop goroutine which regularly refreshes the lease of the key.

```go
type TTLOption struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewTTLOption"></a>
### func [NewTTLOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/etcd/registrar.go#L47>)

```go
func NewTTLOption(heartbeat, ttl time.Duration) *TTLOption
```

NewTTLOption returns a TTLOption that contains proper TTL settings. Heartbeat is used to refresh the lease of the key periodically; its value should be at least 500ms. TTL defines the lease of the key; its value should be significantly greater than heartbeat.

Good default values might be 3s heartbeat, 10s TTL.

# etcdv3

```go
import "github.com/go-kit/kit/sd/etcdv3"
```

Package etcdv3 provides an Instancer and Registrar implementation for etcd v3. If you use etcd v3 as your service discovery system, this package will help you implement the registration and client\-side load balancing patterns.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"io"
	"time"

	"github.com/go-kit/kit/endpoint"
	"github.com/go-kit/kit/sd"
	"github.com/go-kit/kit/sd/lb"
	"github.com/go-kit/log"
	"google.golang.org/grpc"
)

func main() {
	// Let's say this is a service that means to register itself.
	// First, we will set up some context.
	var (
		etcdServer = "10.0.0.1:2379"      // in the change from v2 to v3, the schema is no longer necessary if connecting directly to an etcd v3 instance
		prefix     = "/services/foosvc/"  // known at compile time
		instance   = "1.2.3.4:8080"       // taken from runtime or platform, somehow
		key        = prefix + instance    // should be globally unique
		value      = "http://" + instance // based on our transport
		ctx        = context.Background()
	)

	options := ClientOptions{
		// Path to trusted ca file
		CACert: "",

		// Path to certificate
		Cert: "",

		// Path to private key
		Key: "",

		// Username if required
		Username: "",

		// Password if required
		Password: "",

		// If DialTimeout is 0, it defaults to 3s
		DialTimeout: time.Second * 3,

		// If DialKeepAlive is 0, it defaults to 3s
		DialKeepAlive: time.Second * 3,

		// If passing `grpc.WithBlock`, dial connection will block until success.
		DialOptions: []grpc.DialOption{grpc.WithBlock()},
	}

	// Build the client.
	client, err := NewClient(ctx, []string{etcdServer}, options)
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}

	// Build the registrar.
	registrar := NewRegistrar(client, Service{
		Key:   key,
		Value: value,
	}, log.NewNopLogger())

	// Register our instance.
	registrar.Register()

	// At the end of our service lifecycle, for example at the end of func main,
	// we should make sure to deregister ourselves. This is important! Don't
	// accidentally skip this step by invoking a log.Fatal or os.Exit in the
	// interim, which bypasses the defer stack.
	defer registrar.Deregister()

	// It's likely that we'll also want to connect to other services and call
	// their methods. We can build an Instancer to listen for changes from etcd,
	// create Endpointer, wrap it with a load-balancer to pick a single
	// endpoint, and finally wrap it with a retry strategy to get something that
	// can be used as an endpoint directly.
	barPrefix := "/services/barsvc"
	logger := log.NewNopLogger()
	instancer, err := NewInstancer(client, barPrefix, logger)
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
	endpointer := sd.NewEndpointer(instancer, barFactory, logger)
	balancer := lb.NewRoundRobin(endpointer)
	retry := lb.Retry(3, 3*time.Second, balancer)

	// And now retry can be used like any other endpoint.
	req := struct{}{}
	if _, err = retry(ctx, req); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}

func barFactory(string) (endpoint.Endpoint, io.Closer, error) { return endpoint.Nop, nil, nil }
```

</p>
</details>

# lb

```go
import "github.com/go-kit/kit/sd/lb"
```

Package lb implements the client\-side load balancer pattern. When combined with a service discovery system of record, it enables a more decentralized architecture, removing the need for separate load balancers like HAProxy.

## Variables

<a name="ErrNoEndpoints"></a>ErrNoEndpoints is returned when no qualifying endpoints are available.

```go
var ErrNoEndpoints = errors.New("no endpoints available")
```

<a name="Retry"></a>
## func [Retry](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/lb/retry.go#L44>)

```go
func Retry(max int, timeout time.Duration, b Balancer) endpoint.Endpoint
```

Retry wraps a service load balancer and returns an endpoint oriented load balancer for the specified service method. Requests to the endpoint will be automatically load balanced via the load balancer. Requests that return errors will be retried until they succeed, up to max times, or until the timeout is elapsed, whichever comes first.

<a name="RetryWithCallback"></a>
## func [RetryWithCallback](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/lb/retry.go#L64>)

```go
func RetryWithCallback(timeout time.Duration, b Balancer, cb Callback) endpoint.Endpoint
```

RetryWithCallback wraps a service load balancer and returns an endpoint oriented load balancer for the specified service method. Requests to the endpoint will be automatically load balanced via the load balancer. Requests that return errors will be retried until they succeed, up to max times, until the callback returns false, or until the timeout is elapsed, whichever comes first.

<a name="Balancer"></a>
## type [Balancer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/lb/balancer.go#L10-L12>)

Balancer yields endpoints according to some heuristic.

```go
type Balancer interface {
    Endpoint() (endpoint.Endpoint, error)
}
```

```go
func NewRandom(s sd.Endpointer, seed int64) Balancer
```

NewRandom returns a load balancer that selects services randomly.

<a name="NewRoundRobin"></a>
### func [NewRoundRobin](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/lb/round_robin.go#L11>)

```go
func NewRoundRobin(s sd.Endpointer) Balancer
```

NewRoundRobin returns a load balancer that returns services in sequence.

<a name="Callback"></a>
## type [Callback](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/lb/retry.go#L37>)

Callback is a function that is given the current attempt count and the error received from the underlying endpoint. It should return whether the Retry function should continue trying to get a working endpoint, and a custom error if desired. The error message may be nil, but a true/false is always expected. In all cases, if the replacement error is supplied, the received error will be replaced in the calling context.

```go
type Callback func(n int, received error) (keepTrying bool, replacement error)
```

<a name="RetryError"></a>
## type [RetryError](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/lb/retry.go#L14-L17>)

RetryError is an error wrapper that is used by the retry mechanism. All errors returned by the retry mechanism via its endpoint will be RetryErrors.

```go
type RetryError struct {
    RawErrors []error // all errors encountered from endpoints directly
    Final     error   // the final, terminating error
}
```

<a name="RetryError.Error"></a>
### func \(RetryError\) [Error](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/lb/retry.go#L19>)

```go
func (e RetryError) Error() string
```

## Constants

<a name="DefaultConnectTimeout"></a>

```go
const (
    // DefaultConnectTimeout is the default timeout to establish a connection to
    // a ZooKeeper node.
    DefaultConnectTimeout = 2 * time.Second
    // DefaultSessionTimeout is the default timeout to keep the current
    // ZooKeeper session alive during a temporary disconnect.
    DefaultSessionTimeout = 5 * time.Second
)
```

## Variables

<a name="DefaultACL"></a>DefaultACL is the default ACL to use for creating znodes.

```go
var (
    DefaultACL            = zk.WorldACL(zk.PermAll)
    ErrInvalidCredentials = errors.New("invalid credentials provided")
    ErrClientClosed       = errors.New("client service closed")
    ErrNotRegistered      = errors.New("not registered")
    ErrNodeNotFound       = errors.New("node not found")
)
```

<a name="Client"></a>
## type [Client](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/sd/zk/client.go#L33-L46>)

Client is a wrapper around a lower level ZooKeeper client implementation.

```go
type Client interface {
    // GetEntries should query the provided path in ZooKeeper, place a watch on
    // it and retrieve data from its current child nodes.
    GetEntries(path string) ([]string, <-chan zk.Event, error)
    // CreateParentNodes should try to create the path in case it does not exist
    // yet on ZooKeeper.
    CreateParentNodes(path string) error
    // Register a service with ZooKeeper.
    Register(s *Service) error
    // Deregister a service with ZooKeeper.
    Deregister(s *Service) error
    // Stop should properly shutdown the client implementation
    Stop()
}
```

# opencensus

```go
import "github.com/go-kit/kit/tracing/opencensus"
```

Package opencensus provides Go kit integration to the OpenCensus project. OpenCensus is a single distribution of libraries for metrics and distributed tracing with minimal overhead that allows you to export data to multiple backends. The Go kit OpenCencus package as provided here contains middlewares for tracing.

<a name="EndpointOptions"></a>
## type [EndpointOptions](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opencensus/endpoint_options.go#L10-L28>)

EndpointOptions holds the options for tracing an endpoint

```go
type EndpointOptions struct {
    // IgnoreBusinessError if set to true will not treat a business error
    // identified through the endpoint.Failer interface as a span error.
    IgnoreBusinessError bool

    // Attributes holds the default attributes which will be set on span
    // creation by our Endpoint middleware.
    Attributes []trace.Attribute

    // GetName is an optional function that can set the span name based on the existing name
    // for the endpoint and information in the context.
    //
    // If the function is nil, or the returned name is empty, the existing name for the endpoint is used.
    GetName func(ctx context.Context, name string) string

    // GetAttributes is an optional function that can extract trace attributes
    // from the context and add them to the span.
    GetAttributes func(ctx context.Context) []trace.Attribute
}
```

<a name="TracerOption"></a>
## type [TracerOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opencensus/tracer_options.go#L15>)

```go
type TracerOption func(o *TracerOptions)
```

<a name="IsPublic"></a>
### func [IsPublic](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opencensus/tracer_options.go#L45>)

```go
func IsPublic(isPublic bool) TracerOption
```

IsPublic should be set to true for publicly accessible servers and for clients that should not propagate their current trace metadata. On the server side a new trace will always be started regardless of any trace metadata being found in the incoming request. If any trace metadata is found, it will be added as a linked trace instead.

WithHTTPPropagation sets the propagation handlers for the HTTP transport middlewares. If used on a non HTTP transport this is a noop.

WithName sets the name for an instrumented transport endpoint. If name is omitted at tracing middleware creation, the method of the transport or transport rpc name is used.

# opentracing

```go
import "github.com/go-kit/kit/tracing/opentracing"
```

Package opentracing provides Go kit integration to the OpenTracing project. OpenTracing implements a general purpose interface that microservices can program against, and which adapts to all major distributed tracing systems.

<a name="ContextToGRPC"></a>
## func [ContextToGRPC](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/grpc.go#L18>)

```go
func ContextToGRPC(tracer opentracing.Tracer, logger log.Logger) func(ctx context.Context, md *metadata.MD) context.Context
```

ContextToGRPC returns a grpc RequestFunc that injects an OpenTracing Span found in \`ctx\` into the grpc Metadata. If no such Span can be found, the RequestFunc is a noop.

<a name="ContextToHTTP"></a>
## func [ContextToHTTP](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/http.go#L19>)

```go
func ContextToHTTP(tracer opentracing.Tracer, logger log.Logger) kithttp.RequestFunc
```

ContextToHTTP returns an http RequestFunc that injects an OpenTracing Span found in \`ctx\` into the http headers. If no such Span can be found, the RequestFunc is a noop.

<a name="GRPCToContext"></a>
## func [GRPCToContext](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/grpc.go#L35>)

```go
func GRPCToContext(tracer opentracing.Tracer, operationName string, logger log.Logger) func(ctx context.Context, md metadata.MD) context.Context
```

GRPCToContext returns a grpc RequestFunc that tries to join with an OpenTracing trace found in \`req\` and starts a new Span called \`operationName\` accordingly. If no trace could be found in \`req\`, the Span will be a trace root. The Span is incorporated in the returned Context and can be retrieved with opentracing.SpanFromContext\(ctx\).

<a name="HTTPToContext"></a>
## func [HTTPToContext](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/http.go#L54>)

```go
func HTTPToContext(tracer opentracing.Tracer, operationName string, logger log.Logger) kithttp.RequestFunc
```

HTTPToContext returns an http RequestFunc that tries to join with an OpenTracing trace found in \`req\` and starts a new Span called \`operationName\` accordingly. If no trace could be found in \`req\`, the Span will be a trace root. The Span is incorporated in the returned Context and can be retrieved with opentracing.SpanFromContext\(ctx\).

<a name="TraceClient"></a>
## func [TraceClient](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint.go#L112>)

```go
func TraceClient(tracer opentracing.Tracer, operationName string, opts ...EndpointOption) endpoint.Middleware
```

TraceClient returns a Middleware that wraps the \`next\` Endpoint in an OpenTracing Span called \`operationName\` with client span.kind tag.

<a name="TraceEndpoint"></a>
## func [TraceEndpoint](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint.go#L20>)

```go
func TraceEndpoint(tracer opentracing.Tracer, operationName string, opts ...EndpointOption) endpoint.Middleware
```

TraceEndpoint returns a Middleware that wraps the \`next\` Endpoint in an OpenTracing Span called \`operationName\`.

If \`ctx\` already has a Span, child span is created from it. If \`ctx\` doesn't yet have a Span, the new one is created.

<a name="TraceServer"></a>
## func [TraceServer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint.go#L102>)

```go
func TraceServer(tracer opentracing.Tracer, operationName string, opts ...EndpointOption) endpoint.Middleware
```

TraceServer returns a Middleware that wraps the \`next\` Endpoint in an OpenTracing Span called \`operationName\` with server span.kind tag..

<a name="EndpointOption"></a>
## type [EndpointOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint_options.go#L31>)

EndpointOption allows for functional options to endpoint tracing middleware.

```go
type EndpointOption func(*EndpointOptions)
```

<a name="WithIgnoreBusinessError"></a>
### func [WithIgnoreBusinessError](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint_options.go#L42>)

```go
func WithIgnoreBusinessError(ignoreBusinessError bool) EndpointOption
```

WithIgnoreBusinessError if set to true will not treat a business error identified through the endpoint.Failer interface as a span error.

<a name="WithOperationNameFunc"></a>
### func [WithOperationNameFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint_options.go#L50>)

```go
func WithOperationNameFunc(getOperationName func(ctx context.Context, name string) string) EndpointOption
```

WithOperationNameFunc allows to set function that can set the span operation name based on the existing one for the endpoint and information in the context.

<a name="WithOptions"></a>
### func [WithOptions](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint_options.go#L34>)

```go
func WithOptions(options EndpointOptions) EndpointOption
```

WithOptions sets all configuration options at once by use of the EndpointOptions struct.

<a name="WithTags"></a>
### func [WithTags](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint_options.go#L57>)

```go
func WithTags(tags opentracing.Tags) EndpointOption
```

WithTags adds default tags for the spans created by the Endpoint tracer.

<a name="WithTagsFunc"></a>
### func [WithTagsFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint_options.go#L70>)

```go
func WithTagsFunc(getTags func(ctx context.Context) opentracing.Tags) EndpointOption
```

WithTagsFunc set the func to extracts additional tags from the context.

<a name="EndpointOptions"></a>
## type [EndpointOptions](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/opentracing/endpoint_options.go#L10-L28>)

EndpointOptions holds the options for tracing an endpoint

```go
type EndpointOptions struct {
    // IgnoreBusinessError if set to true will not treat a business error
    // identified through the endpoint.Failer interface as a span error.
    IgnoreBusinessError bool

    // GetOperationName is an optional function that can set the span operation name based on the existing one
    // for the endpoint and information in the context.
    //
    // If the function is nil, or the returned name is empty, the existing name for the endpoint is used.
    GetOperationName func(ctx context.Context, name string) string

    // Tags holds the default tags which will be set on span
    // creation by our Endpoint middleware.
    Tags opentracing.Tags

    // GetTags is an optional function that can extract tags
    // from the context and add them to the span.
    GetTags func(ctx context.Context) opentracing.Tags
}
```

# zipkin

```go
import "github.com/go-kit/kit/tracing/zipkin"
```

Package zipkin provides Go kit integration to the OpenZipkin project through the use of zipkin\-go, the official OpenZipkin tracer implementation for Go. OpenZipkin is the most used open source distributed tracing ecosystem with many different libraries and interoperability options.

## Index

- [func GRPCClientTrace\(tracer \*zipkin.Tracer, options ...TracerOption\) kitgrpc.ClientOption](<#GRPCClientTrace>)
- [func GRPCServerTrace\(tracer \*zipkin.Tracer, options ...TracerOption\) kitgrpc.ServerOption](<#GRPCServerTrace>)
- [func HTTPClientTrace\(tracer \*zipkin.Tracer, options ...TracerOption\) kithttp.ClientOption](<#HTTPClientTrace>)
- [func HTTPServerTrace\(tracer \*zipkin.Tracer, options ...TracerOption\) kithttp.ServerOption](<#HTTPServerTrace>)
- [func TraceEndpoint\(tracer \*zipkin.Tracer, name string\) endpoint.Middleware](<#TraceEndpoint>)
- [type TracerOption](<#TracerOption>)
  - [func AllowPropagation\(propagate bool\) TracerOption](<#AllowPropagation>)
  - [func Logger\(logger log.Logger\) TracerOption](<#Logger>)
  - [func Name\(name string\) TracerOption](<#Name>)
  - [func RequestSampler\(sampleFunc func\(r \*http.Request\) bool\) TracerOption](<#RequestSampler>)
  - [func Tags\(tags map\[string\]string\) TracerOption](<#Tags>)


<a name="GRPCClientTrace"></a>
## func [GRPCClientTrace](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/grpc.go#L29>)

```go
func GRPCClientTrace(tracer *zipkin.Tracer, options ...TracerOption) kitgrpc.ClientOption
```

GRPCClientTrace enables native Zipkin tracing of a Go kit gRPC transport Client.

Go kit creates gRPC transport clients per remote endpoint. This middleware can be set\-up individually by adding the endpoint name for each of the Go kit transport clients using the Name\(\) TracerOption. If wanting to use the gRPC FullMethod \(/service/method\) as Span name you can create a global client tracer omitting the Name\(\) TracerOption, which you can then feed to each Go kit gRPC transport client. If instrumenting a client to an external \(not on your platform\) service, you will probably want to disallow propagation of SpanContext using the AllowPropagation TracerOption and setting it to false.

<a name="GRPCServerTrace"></a>
## func [GRPCServerTrace](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/grpc.go#L123>)

```go
func GRPCServerTrace(tracer *zipkin.Tracer, options ...TracerOption) kitgrpc.ServerOption
```

GRPCServerTrace enables native Zipkin tracing of a Go kit gRPC transport Server.

Go kit creates gRPC transport servers per gRPC method. This middleware can be set\-up individually by adding the method name for each of the Go kit method servers using the Name\(\) TracerOption. If wanting to use the gRPC FullMethod \(/service/method\) as Span name you can create a global server tracer omitting the Name\(\) TracerOption, which you can then feed to each Go kit method server. For this to work you will need to wire the Go kit gRPC Interceptor too. If instrumenting a service to external \(not on your platform\) clients, you will probably want to disallow propagation of a client SpanContext using the AllowPropagation TracerOption and setting it to false.

<a name="HTTPClientTrace"></a>
## func [HTTPClientTrace](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/http.go#L28>)

```go
func HTTPClientTrace(tracer *zipkin.Tracer, options ...TracerOption) kithttp.ClientOption
```

HTTPClientTrace enables native Zipkin tracing of a Go kit HTTP transport Client.

Go kit creates HTTP transport clients per remote endpoint. This middleware can be set\-up individually by adding the endpoint name for each of the Go kit transport clients using the Name\(\) TracerOption. If wanting to use the HTTP Method \(Get, Post, Put, etc.\) as Span name you can create a global client tracer omitting the Name\(\) TracerOption, which you can then feed to each Go kit transport client. If instrumenting a client to an external \(not on your platform\) service, you will probably want to disallow propagation of SpanContext using the AllowPropagation TracerOption and setting it to false.

<a name="HTTPServerTrace"></a>
## func [HTTPServerTrace](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/http.go#L132>)

```go
func HTTPServerTrace(tracer *zipkin.Tracer, options ...TracerOption) kithttp.ServerOption
```

HTTPServerTrace enables native Zipkin tracing of a Go kit HTTP transport Server.

Go kit creates HTTP transport servers per HTTP endpoint. This middleware can be set\-up individually by adding the method name for each of the Go kit method servers using the Name\(\) TracerOption. If wanting to use the HTTP method \(Get, Post, Put, etc.\) as Span name you can create a global server tracer omitting the Name\(\) TracerOption, which you can then feed to each Go kit method server.

If instrumenting a service to external \(not on your platform\) clients, you will probably want to disallow propagation of a client SpanContext using the AllowPropagation TracerOption and setting it to false.

<a name="TraceEndpoint"></a>
## func [TraceEndpoint](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/endpoint.go#L16>)

```go
func TraceEndpoint(tracer *zipkin.Tracer, name string) endpoint.Middleware
```

TraceEndpoint returns an Endpoint middleware, tracing a Go kit endpoint. This endpoint tracer should be used in combination with a Go kit Transport tracing middleware or custom before and after transport functions as propagation of SpanContext is not provided in this middleware.

<a name="TracerOption"></a>
## type [TracerOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/options.go#L10>)

TracerOption allows for functional options to our Zipkin tracing middleware.

```go
type TracerOption func(o *tracerOptions)
```

<a name="AllowPropagation"></a>
### func [AllowPropagation](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/options.go#L47>)

```go
func AllowPropagation(propagate bool) TracerOption
```

AllowPropagation instructs the tracer to allow or deny propagation of the span context between this instrumented client or service and its peers. If the instrumented client connects to services outside its own platform or if the instrumented service receives requests from untrusted clients it is strongly advised to disallow propagation. Propagation between services inside your own platform benefit from propagation. Default for both TraceClient and TraceServer is to allow propagation.

<a name="Logger"></a>
### func [Logger](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/options.go#L32>)

```go
func Logger(logger log.Logger) TracerOption
```

Logger adds a Go kit logger to our Zipkin Middleware to log SpanContext extract / inject errors if they occur. Default is Noop.

<a name="Name"></a>
### func [Name](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/options.go#L15>)

```go
func Name(name string) TracerOption
```

Name sets the name for an instrumented transport endpoint. If name is omitted at tracing middleware creation, the method of the transport or transport rpc name is used.

<a name="RequestSampler"></a>
### func [RequestSampler](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/options.go#L55>)

```go
func RequestSampler(sampleFunc func(r *http.Request) bool) TracerOption
```

RequestSampler allows one to set the sampling decision based on the details found in the http.Request.

<a name="Tags"></a>
### func [Tags](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/tracing/zipkin/options.go#L22>)

```go
func Tags(tags map[string]string) TracerOption
```

Tags adds default tags to our Zipkin transport spans.

<a name="DefaultDeliverer"></a>
## func [DefaultDeliverer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/publisher.go#L124-L128>)

```go
func DefaultDeliverer(ctx context.Context, p Publisher, pub *amqp.Publishing) (*amqp.Delivery, error)
```

DefaultDeliverer is a deliverer that publishes the specified Publishing and returns the first Delivery object with the matching correlationId. If the context times out while waiting for a reply, an error will be returned.

<a name="DefaultErrorEncoder"></a>
## func [DefaultErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L226-L227>)

```go
func DefaultErrorEncoder(ctx context.Context, err error, deliv *amqp.Delivery, ch Channel, pub *amqp.Publishing)
```

DefaultErrorEncoder simply ignores the message. It does not reply nor Ack/Nack the message.

<a name="DefaultResponsePublisher"></a>
## func [DefaultResponsePublisher](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L180-L185>)

```go
func DefaultResponsePublisher(ctx context.Context, deliv *amqp.Delivery, ch Channel, pub *amqp.Publishing) error
```

DefaultResponsePublisher extracts the reply exchange and reply key from the request, and sends the response object to that destination.

<a name="EncodeJSONResponse"></a>
## func [EncodeJSONResponse](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L144-L148>)

```go
func EncodeJSONResponse(ctx context.Context, pub *amqp.Publishing, response interface{}) error
```

EncodeJSONResponse marshals the response as JSON as part of the payload of the AMQP Publishing object.

<a name="EncodeNopResponse"></a>
## func [EncodeNopResponse](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L158-L162>)

```go
func EncodeNopResponse(ctx context.Context, pub *amqp.Publishing, response interface{}) error
```

EncodeNopResponse is a response function that does nothing.

<a name="NopResponsePublisher"></a>
## func [NopResponsePublisher](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L208-L213>)

```go
func NopResponsePublisher(ctx context.Context, deliv *amqp.Delivery, ch Channel, pub *amqp.Publishing) error
```

NopResponsePublisher does not deliver a response to the original sender. This response publisher is used when the user wants the subscriber to receive and forget.

<a name="ReplyAndAckErrorEncoder"></a>
## func [ReplyAndAckErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L282>)

```go
func ReplyAndAckErrorEncoder(ctx context.Context, err error, deliv *amqp.Delivery, ch Channel, pub *amqp.Publishing)
```

ReplyAndAckErrorEncoder serializes the error message as a DefaultErrorResponse JSON and sends the message to the ReplyTo address then Acks the original message.

<a name="ReplyErrorEncoder"></a>
## func [ReplyErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L244-L250>)

```go
func ReplyErrorEncoder(ctx context.Context, err error, deliv *amqp.Delivery, ch Channel, pub *amqp.Publishing)
```

ReplyErrorEncoder serializes the error message as a DefaultErrorResponse JSON and sends the message to the ReplyTo address.

<a name="SendAndForgetDeliverer"></a>
## func [SendAndForgetDeliverer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/publisher.go#L175-L179>)

```go
func SendAndForgetDeliverer(ctx context.Context, p Publisher, pub *amqp.Publishing) (*amqp.Delivery, error)
```

SendAndForgetDeliverer delivers the supplied publishing and returns a nil response. When using this deliverer please ensure that the supplied DecodeResponseFunc and PublisherResponseFunc are able to handle nil\-type responses.

<a name="SingleNackRequeueErrorEncoder"></a>
## func [SingleNackRequeueErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L232-L233>)

```go
func SingleNackRequeueErrorEncoder(ctx context.Context, err error, deliv *amqp.Delivery, ch Channel, pub *amqp.Publishing)
```

SingleNackRequeueErrorEncoder issues a Nack to the delivery with multiple flag set as false and requeue flag set as true. It does not reply the message.

<a name="Channel"></a>
## type [Channel](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L295-L298>)

Channel is a channel interface to make testing possible. It is highly recommended to use \*amqp.Channel as the interface implementation.

```go
type Channel interface {
    Publish(exchange, key string, mandatory, immediate bool, msg amqp.Publishing) error
    Consume(queue, consumer string, autoAck, exclusive, noLocal, noWail bool, args amqp.Table) (<-chan amqp.Delivery, error)
}
```

EncodeResponseFunc encodes the passed response object to an AMQP Publishing object. It is designed to be used in AMQP Subscribers.

```go
type EncodeResponseFunc func(context.Context, *amqp.Publishing, interface{}) error
```

<a name="ErrorEncoder"></a>
## type [ErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L221-L222>)

ErrorEncoder is responsible for encoding an error to the subscriber reply. Users are encouraged to use custom ErrorEncoders to encode errors to their replies, and will likely want to pass and check for their own error types.

```go
    err error, deliv *amqp.Delivery, ch Channel, pub *amqp.Publishing)
```

Publisher wraps an AMQP channel and queue, and provides a method that implements endpoint.Endpoint.

```go
}
```


NewPublisher constructs a usable Publisher for a single remote method.

```go

```

## type [PublisherOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/publisher.go#L51>)

PublisherOption sets an optional parameter for clients.

```go
type PublisherOption func(*Publisher)
```

<a name="PublisherAfter"></a>
### func [PublisherAfter](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/publisher.go#L62>)

```go
func PublisherAfter(after ...PublisherResponseFunc) PublisherOption
```

PublisherAfter sets the ClientResponseFuncs applied to the incoming AMQP request prior to it being decoded. This is useful for obtaining anything off of the response and adding onto the context prior to decoding.

<a name="PublisherBefore"></a>
### func [PublisherBefore](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/publisher.go#L55>)

```go
func PublisherBefore(before ...RequestFunc) PublisherOption
```

PublisherBefore sets the RequestFuncs that are applied to the outgoing AMQP request before it's invoked.

<a name="PublisherDeliverer"></a>
### func [PublisherDeliverer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/publisher.go#L67>)

```go

```

```go

```

PublisherTimeout sets the available timeout for an AMQP request.

<a name="PublisherResponseFunc"></a>
## type [PublisherResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L27>)

PublisherResponseFunc may take information from an AMQP request and make the response available for consumption. PublisherResponseFunc are only executed in publishers, after a request has been made, but prior to it being decoded.

```go
type PublisherResponseFunc func(context.Context, *amqp.Delivery) context.Context
```

<a name="RequestFunc"></a>
## type [RequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L13>)

RequestFunc may take information from a publisher request and put it into a request context. In Subscribers, RequestFuncs are executed prior to invoking the endpoint.

```go
type RequestFunc func(context.Context, *amqp.Publishing, *amqp.Delivery) context.Context
```

<a name="SetConsumeArgs"></a>
### func [SetConsumeArgs](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L79>)

```go
func SetConsumeArgs(args amqp.Table) RequestFunc
```

SetConsumeArgs returns a RequestFunc that set the arguments for amqp Consume function. It is designed to be used by Publishers.

<a name="SetConsumeAutoAck"></a>
### func [SetConsumeAutoAck](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L70>)

```go
func SetConsumeAutoAck(autoAck bool) RequestFunc
```

SetConsumeAutoAck returns a RequestFunc that sets whether or not to autoAck messages when consuming. When set to false, the publisher will Ack the first message it receives with a matching correlationId. It is designed to be used by Publishers.

<a name="SetContentEncoding"></a>
### func [SetContentEncoding](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L96>)

```go
func SetContentEncoding(contentEncoding string) RequestFunc
```

SetContentEncoding returns a RequestFunc that sets the ContentEncoding field of an AMQP Publishing.

<a name="SetContentType"></a>
### func [SetContentType](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L87>)

```go
func SetContentType(contentType string) RequestFunc
```

SetContentType returns a RequestFunc that sets the ContentType field of an AMQP Publishing.

<a name="SetCorrelationID"></a>
### func [SetCorrelationID](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L105>)

```go
func SetCorrelationID(cid string) RequestFunc
```

SetCorrelationID returns a RequestFunc that sets the CorrelationId field of an AMQP Publishing.

<a name="SetNackSleepDuration"></a>
### func [SetNackSleepDuration](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L59>)

```go
func SetNackSleepDuration(duration time.Duration) RequestFunc
```

SetNackSleepDuration returns a RequestFunc that sets the amount of time to sleep in the event of a Nack. This has to be used in conjunction with an error encoder that Nack and sleeps. One example is the SingleNackRequeueErrorEncoder. It is designed to be used by Subscribers.

<a name="SetPublishDeliveryMode"></a>
### func [SetPublishDeliveryMode](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L47>)

```go
func SetPublishDeliveryMode(dmode uint8) RequestFunc
```

SetPublishDeliveryMode sets the delivery mode of a Publishing. Please refer to AMQP delivery mode constants in the AMQP package.

<a name="SetPublishExchange"></a>
### func [SetPublishExchange](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L31>)

```go
func SetPublishExchange(publishExchange string) RequestFunc
```

SetPublishExchange returns a RequestFunc that sets the Exchange field of an AMQP Publish call.

<a name="SetPublishKey"></a>
### func [SetPublishKey](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L39>)

```go
func SetPublishKey(publishKey string) RequestFunc
```

SetPublishKey returns a RequestFunc that sets the Key field of an AMQP Publish call.

<a name="ResponsePublisher"></a>
## type [ResponsePublisher](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L171-L176>)

ResponsePublisher functions are executed by the subscriber to publish response object to the original sender. Please note that the word "publisher" does not refer to the publisher of pub/sub. Rather, publisher is merely a function that publishes, or sends responses.

```go
type ResponsePublisher func(
    context.Context,
    *amqp.Delivery,
    Channel,
    *amqp.Publishing,
) error
```

<a name="Subscriber"></a>
## type [Subscriber](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L15-L24>)

Subscriber wraps an endpoint and provides a handler for AMQP Delivery messages.

```go
type Subscriber struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewSubscriber"></a>
### func [NewSubscriber](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L28-L33>)

```go
func NewSubscriber(e endpoint.Endpoint, dec DecodeRequestFunc, enc EncodeResponseFunc, options ...SubscriberOption) *Subscriber
```

NewSubscriber constructs a new subscriber, which provides a handler for AMQP Delivery messages.

<a name="Subscriber.ServeDelivery"></a>
### func \(Subscriber\) [ServeDelivery](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L98>)

```go
func (s Subscriber) ServeDelivery(ch Channel) func(deliv *amqp.Delivery)
```

ServeDelivery handles AMQP Delivery messages It is strongly recommended to use \*amqp.Channel as the Channel interface implementation.

<a name="SubscriberOption"></a>
## type [SubscriberOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L49>)

SubscriberOption sets an optional parameter for subscribers.

```go
type SubscriberOption func(*Subscriber)
```

<a name="SubscriberAfter"></a>
### func [SubscriberAfter](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L59>)

```go
func SubscriberAfter(after ...SubscriberResponseFunc) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberAfter functions are executed on the subscriber reply after the endpoint is invoked, but before anything is published to the reply.

<a name="SubscriberBefore"></a>
### func [SubscriberBefore](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L53>)

```go
func SubscriberBefore(before ...RequestFunc) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberBefore functions are executed on the publisher delivery object before the request is decoded.

<a name="SubscriberErrorEncoder"></a>
### func [SubscriberErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L74>)

```go
func SubscriberErrorEncoder(ee ErrorEncoder) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberErrorEncoder is used to encode errors to the subscriber reply whenever they're encountered in the processing of a request. Clients can use this to provide custom error formatting. By default, errors will be published with the DefaultErrorEncoder.

<a name="SubscriberErrorHandler"></a>
### func [SubscriberErrorHandler](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L91>)

```go
func SubscriberErrorHandler(errorHandler transport.ErrorHandler) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberErrorHandler is used to handle non\-terminal errors. By default, non\-terminal errors are ignored. This is intended as a diagnostic measure. Finer\-grained control of error handling, including logging in more detail, should be performed in a custom SubscriberErrorEncoder which has access to the context.

<a name="SubscriberErrorLogger"></a>
### func [SubscriberErrorLogger](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L83>)

```go
func SubscriberErrorLogger(logger log.Logger) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberErrorLogger is used to log non\-terminal errors. By default, no errors are logged. This is intended as a diagnostic measure. Finer\-grained control of error handling, including logging in more detail, should be performed in a custom SubscriberErrorEncoder which has access to the context. Deprecated: Use SubscriberErrorHandler instead.

<a name="SubscriberResponsePublisher"></a>
### func [SubscriberResponsePublisher](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/subscriber.go#L66>)

```go
func SubscriberResponsePublisher(rp ResponsePublisher) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberResponsePublisher is used by the subscriber to deliver response objects to the original sender. By default, the DefaultResponsePublisher is used.

<a name="SubscriberResponseFunc"></a>
## type [SubscriberResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L18-L22>)

SubscriberResponseFunc may take information from a request context and use it to manipulate a Publisher. SubscriberResponseFuncs are only executed in subscribers, after invoking the endpoint but prior to publishing a reply.

```go
type SubscriberResponseFunc func(context.Context,
    *amqp.Delivery,
    Channel,
    *amqp.Publishing,
) context.Context
```

<a name="SetAckAfterEndpoint"></a>
### func [SetAckAfterEndpoint](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/amqp/request_response_func.go#L116>)

```go
func SetAckAfterEndpoint(multiple bool) SubscriberResponseFunc
```

SetAckAfterEndpoint returns a SubscriberResponseFunc that prompts the service to Ack the Delivery object after successfully evaluating the endpoint, and before it encodes the response. It is designed to be used by Subscribers.



EncodeKeyValue sanitizes a key\-value pair for use in gRPC metadata headers.

<a name="Interceptor"></a>
## func [Interceptor](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/server.go#L163-L165>)

```go
func Interceptor(ctx context.Context, req interface{}, info *grpc.UnaryServerInfo, handler grpc.UnaryHandler) (resp interface{}, err error)
```

Interceptor is a grpc UnaryInterceptor that injects the method name into context so it can be consumed by Go kit gRPC middlewares. The Interceptor typically is added at creation time of the grpc\-go server. Like this: \`grpc.NewServer\(grpc.UnaryInterceptor\(kitgrpc.Interceptor\)\)\`

<a name="Client"></a>
## type [Client](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/client.go#L16-L26>)

Client wraps a gRPC connection and provides a method that implements endpoint.Endpoint.

```go
type Client struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewClient"></a>
### func [NewClient](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/client.go#L31-L39>)

```go
func NewClient(cc *grpc.ClientConn, serviceName string, method string, enc EncodeRequestFunc, dec DecodeResponseFunc, grpcReply interface{}, options ...ClientOption) *Client
```

NewClient constructs a usable Client for a single remote endpoint. Pass an zero\-value protobuf message of the RPC response type as the grpcReply argument.

<a name="Client.Endpoint"></a>
### func \(Client\) [Endpoint](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/client.go#L87>)

```go
func (c Client) Endpoint() endpoint.Endpoint
```

Endpoint returns a usable endpoint that will invoke the gRPC specified by the client.

<a name="ClientFinalizerFunc"></a>
## type [ClientFinalizerFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/client.go#L140>)

ClientFinalizerFunc can be used to perform work at the end of a client gRPC request, after the response is returned. The principal intended use is for error logging. Additional response parameters are provided in the context under keys with the ContextKeyResponse prefix. Note: err may be nil. There maybe also no additional response parameters depending on when an error occurs.

```go
type ClientFinalizerFunc func(ctx context.Context, err error)
```

<a name="ClientOption"></a>
## type [ClientOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/client.go#L64>)

ClientOption sets an optional parameter for clients.

```go
type ClientOption func(*Client)
```

<a name="ClientAfter"></a>
### func [ClientAfter](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/client.go#L75>)

```go
func ClientAfter(after ...ClientResponseFunc) ClientOption
```

ClientAfter sets the ClientResponseFuncs that are applied to the incoming gRPC response prior to it being decoded. This is useful for obtaining response metadata and adding onto the context prior to decoding.

<a name="ClientBefore"></a>
### func [ClientBefore](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/client.go#L68>)

```go
func ClientBefore(before ...ClientRequestFunc) ClientOption
```

ClientBefore sets the RequestFuncs that are applied to the outgoing gRPC request before it's invoked.

<a name="ClientFinalizer"></a>
### func [ClientFinalizer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/client.go#L81>)

```go
func ClientFinalizer(f ...ClientFinalizerFunc) ClientOption
```

ClientFinalizer is executed at the end of every gRPC request. By default, no finalizer is registered.

<a name="ClientRequestFunc"></a>
## type [ClientRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/request_response_funcs.go#L19>)

ClientRequestFunc may take information from context and use it to construct metadata headers to be transported to the server. ClientRequestFuncs are executed after creating the request but prior to sending the gRPC request to the server.

```go
type ClientRequestFunc func(context.Context, *metadata.MD) context.Context
```

<a name="SetRequestHeader"></a>
### func [SetRequestHeader](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/request_response_funcs.go#L40>)

```go
func SetRequestHeader(key, val string) ClientRequestFunc
```

SetRequestHeader returns a ClientRequestFunc that sets the specified metadata key\-value pair.

<a name="ClientResponseFunc"></a>
## type [ClientResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/request_response_funcs.go#L36>)

ClientResponseFunc may take information from a gRPC metadata header and/or trailer and make the responses available for consumption. ClientResponseFuncs are only executed in clients, after a request has been made, but prior to it being decoded.

```go
type ClientResponseFunc func(ctx context.Context, header metadata.MD, trailer metadata.MD) context.Context
```

<a name="DecodeRequestFunc"></a>
## type [DecodeRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/encode_decode.go#L11>)

DecodeRequestFunc extracts a user\-domain request object from a gRPC request. It's designed to be used in gRPC servers, for server\-side endpoints. One straightforward DecodeRequestFunc could be something that decodes from the gRPC request message to the concrete request type.

```go
type DecodeRequestFunc func(context.Context, interface{}) (request interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="DecodeResponseFunc"></a>
## type [DecodeResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/encode_decode.go#L29>)

DecodeResponseFunc extracts a user\-domain response object from a gRPC response object. It's designed to be used in gRPC clients, for client\-side endpoints. One straightforward DecodeResponseFunc could be something that decodes from the gRPC response message to the concrete response type.

```go
type DecodeResponseFunc func(context.Context, interface{}) (response interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="EncodeRequestFunc"></a>
## type [EncodeRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/encode_decode.go#L17>)

EncodeRequestFunc encodes the passed request object into the gRPC request object. It's designed to be used in gRPC clients, for client\-side endpoints. One straightforward EncodeRequestFunc could something that encodes the object directly to the gRPC request message.

```go
type EncodeRequestFunc func(context.Context, interface{}) (request interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="EncodeResponseFunc"></a>
## type [EncodeResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/encode_decode.go#L23>)

EncodeResponseFunc encodes the passed response object to the gRPC response message. It's designed to be used in gRPC servers, for server\-side endpoints. One straightforward EncodeResponseFunc could be something that encodes the object directly to the gRPC response message.

```go
type EncodeResponseFunc func(context.Context, interface{}) (response interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="Handler"></a>
## type [Handler](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/server.go#L17-L19>)

Handler which should be called from the gRPC binding of the service implementation. The incoming request parameter, and returned response parameter, are both gRPC types, not user\-domain.

```go
type Handler interface {
    ServeGRPC(ctx context.Context, request interface{}) (context.Context, interface{}, error)
}
```

<a name="Server"></a>
## type [Server](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/server.go#L22-L30>)

Server wraps an endpoint and implements grpc.Handler.

```go
type Server struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewServer"></a>
### func [NewServer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/server.go#L37-L42>)

```go
func NewServer(e endpoint.Endpoint, dec DecodeRequestFunc, enc EncodeResponseFunc, options ...ServerOption) *Server
```

NewServer constructs a new server, which implements wraps the provided endpoint and implements the Handler interface. Consumers should write bindings that adapt the concrete gRPC methods from their compiled protobuf definitions to individual handlers. Request and response objects are from the caller business domain, not gRPC request and reply types.

ServerAfter functions are executed on the gRPC response writer after the endpoint is invoked, but before anything is written to the client.

<a name="ServerErrorLogger"></a>
### func [ServerErrorLogger](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/server.go#L73>)

```go
func ServerErrorLogger(logger log.Logger) ServerOption
```

ServerErrorLogger is used to log non\-terminal errors. By default, no errors are logged. Deprecated: Use ServerErrorHandler instead.

<a name="ServerFinalizer"></a>
### func [ServerFinalizer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/server.go#L85>)

```go
func ServerFinalizer(f ...ServerFinalizerFunc) ServerOption
```

ServerFinalizer is executed at the end of every gRPC request. By default, no finalizer is registered.

<a name="ServerRequestFunc"></a>
## type [ServerRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/request_response_funcs.go#L24>)

ServerRequestFunc may take information from the received metadata header and use it to place items in the request scoped context. ServerRequestFuncs are executed prior to invoking the endpoint.

```go
type ServerRequestFunc func(context.Context, metadata.MD) context.Context
```

<a name="ServerResponseFunc"></a>
## type [ServerResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/request_response_funcs.go#L30>)

ServerResponseFunc may take information from a request context and use it to manipulate the gRPC response metadata headers and trailers. ResponseFuncs are only executed in servers, after invoking the endpoint but prior to writing a response.

```go
type ServerResponseFunc func(ctx context.Context, header *metadata.MD, trailer *metadata.MD) context.Context
```

<a name="DefaultErrorEncoder"></a>
## func [DefaultErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L190>)

```go
func DefaultErrorEncoder(_ context.Context, err error, w http.ResponseWriter)
```

DefaultErrorEncoder writes the error to the ResponseWriter, by default a content type of text/plain, a body of the plain text of the error, and a status code of 500. If the error implements Headerer, the provided headers will be applied to the response. If the error implements json.Marshaler, and the marshaling succeeds, a content type of application/json and the JSON encoded form of the error will be used. If the error implements StatusCoder, the provided StatusCode will be used instead of 500.

<a name="EncodeJSONRequest"></a>
## func [EncodeJSONRequest](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L175>)

```go
func EncodeJSONRequest(c context.Context, r *http.Request, request interface{}) error
```

EncodeJSONRequest is an EncodeRequestFunc that serializes the request as a JSON object to the Request body. Many JSON\-over\-HTTP services can use it as a sensible default. If the request implements Headerer, the provided headers will be applied to the request.

<a name="EncodeJSONResponse"></a>
## func [EncodeJSONResponse](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L163>)

```go
func EncodeJSONResponse(_ context.Context, w http.ResponseWriter, response interface{}) error
```

EncodeJSONResponse is a EncodeResponseFunc that serializes the response as a JSON object to the ResponseWriter. Many JSON\-over\-HTTP services can use it as a sensible default. If the response implements Headerer, the provided headers will be applied to the response. If the response implements StatusCoder, the provided StatusCode will be used instead of 200.

<a name="EncodeXMLRequest"></a>
## func [EncodeXMLRequest](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L190>)

```go
func EncodeXMLRequest(c context.Context, r *http.Request, request interface{}) error
```

EncodeXMLRequest is an EncodeRequestFunc that serializes the request as a XML object to the Request body. If the request implements Headerer, the provided headers will be applied to the request.

<a name="NopRequestDecoder"></a>
## func [NopRequestDecoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L154>)

```go
func NopRequestDecoder(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request) (interface{}, error)
```

NopRequestDecoder is a DecodeRequestFunc that can be used for requests that do not need to be decoded, and simply returns nil, nil.

<a name="PopulateRequestContext"></a>
## func [PopulateRequestContext](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/request_response_funcs.go#L49>)

```go
func PopulateRequestContext(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request) context.Context
```

PopulateRequestContext is a RequestFunc that populates several values into the context from the HTTP request. Those values may be extracted using the corresponding ContextKey type in this package.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
handler := NewServer(
	func(ctx context.Context, request interface{}) (response interface{}, err error) {
		fmt.Println("Method", ctx.Value(ContextKeyRequestMethod).(string))
		fmt.Println("RequestPath", ctx.Value(ContextKeyRequestPath).(string))
		fmt.Println("RequestURI", ctx.Value(ContextKeyRequestURI).(string))
		fmt.Println("X-Request-ID", ctx.Value(ContextKeyRequestXRequestID).(string))
		return struct{}{}, nil
	},
	func(context.Context, *http.Request) (interface{}, error) { return struct{}{}, nil },
	func(context.Context, http.ResponseWriter, interface{}) error { return nil },
	ServerBefore(PopulateRequestContext),
)

server := httptest.NewServer(handler)
defer server.Close()

req, _ := http.NewRequest("PATCH", fmt.Sprintf("%s/search?q=sympatico", server.URL), nil)
req.Header.Set("X-Request-Id", "a1b2c3d4e5")
http.DefaultClient.Do(req)

// Output:
// Method PATCH
// RequestPath /search
// RequestURI /search?q=sympatico
// X-Request-ID a1b2c3d4e5
```

#### Output

```
Method PATCH
RequestPath /search
RequestURI /search?q=sympatico
X-Request-ID a1b2c3d4e5
```

</p>
</details>

Client wraps a URL and provides a method that implements endpoint.Endpoint.

```go
type Client struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go
func NewClient(method string, tgt *url.URL, enc EncodeRequestFunc, dec DecodeResponseFunc, options ...ClientOption) *Client
```

NewClient constructs a usable Client for a single remote method.

### func [NewExplicitClient](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L40>)

```go
func NewExplicitClient(req CreateRequestFunc, dec DecodeResponseFunc, options ...ClientOption) *Client
```

<a name="ClientFinalizerFunc"></a>
## type [ClientFinalizerFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L169>)

ClientFinalizerFunc can be used to perform work at the end of a client HTTP request, after the response is returned. The principal intended use is for error logging. Additional response parameters are provided in the context under keys with the ContextKeyResponse prefix. Note: err may be nil. There maybe also no additional response parameters depending on when an error occurs.

```go
type ClientFinalizerFunc func(ctx context.Context, err error)
```

<a name="ClientOption"></a>
## type [ClientOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L53>)

ClientOption sets an optional parameter for clients.

```go
type ClientOption func(*Client)
```

<a name="BufferedStream"></a>
### func [BufferedStream](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L85>)

```go
func BufferedStream(buffered bool) ClientOption
```

BufferedStream sets whether the HTTP response body is left open, allowing it to be read from later. Useful for transporting a file as a buffered stream. That body has to be drained and closed to properly end the request.

<a name="ClientAfter"></a>
### func [ClientAfter](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L71>)

```go
func ClientAfter(after ...ClientResponseFunc) ClientOption
```

ClientAfter adds one or more ClientResponseFuncs, which are applied to the incoming HTTP response prior to it being decoded. This is useful for obtaining anything off of the response and adding it into the context prior to decoding.

<a name="ClientBefore"></a>
### func [ClientBefore](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L63>)

```go
func ClientBefore(before ...RequestFunc) ClientOption
```

ClientBefore adds one or more RequestFuncs to be applied to the outgoing HTTP request before it's invoked.

<a name="ClientFinalizer"></a>
### func [ClientFinalizer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L78>)

```go
func ClientFinalizer(f ...ClientFinalizerFunc) ClientOption
```

ClientFinalizer adds one or more ClientFinalizerFuncs to be executed at the end of every HTTP request. Finalizers are executed in the order in which they were added. By default, no finalizer is registered.

<a name="SetClient"></a>
### func [SetClient](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L57>)

```go
func SetClient(client HTTPClient) ClientOption
```

SetClient sets the underlying HTTP client used for requests. By default, http.DefaultClient is used.

<a name="ClientResponseFunc"></a>
## type [ClientResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/request_response_funcs.go#L22>)

ClientResponseFunc may take information from an HTTP request and make the response available for consumption. ClientResponseFuncs are only executed in clients, after a request has been made, but prior to it being decoded.

```go
type ClientResponseFunc func(context.Context, *http.Response) context.Context
```

<a name="CreateRequestFunc"></a>
## type [CreateRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/encode_decode.go#L24>)

CreateRequestFunc creates an outgoing HTTP request based on the passed request object. It's designed to be used in HTTP clients, for client\-side endpoints. It's a more powerful version of EncodeRequestFunc, and can be used if more fine\-grained control of the HTTP request is required.

```go
type CreateRequestFunc func(context.Context, interface{}) (*http.Request, error)
```

<a name="DecodeRequestFunc"></a>
## type [DecodeRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/encode_decode.go#L12>)

DecodeRequestFunc extracts a user\-domain request object from an HTTP request object. It's designed to be used in HTTP servers, for server\-side endpoints. One straightforward DecodeRequestFunc could be something that JSON decodes from the request body to the concrete request type.

```go
type DecodeRequestFunc func(context.Context, *http.Request) (request interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="DecodeResponseFunc"></a>
## type [DecodeResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/encode_decode.go#L36>)

DecodeResponseFunc extracts a user\-domain response object from an HTTP response object. It's designed to be used in HTTP clients, for client\-side endpoints. One straightforward DecodeResponseFunc could be something that JSON decodes from the response body to the concrete response type.

```go
type DecodeResponseFunc func(context.Context, *http.Response) (response interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="EncodeRequestFunc"></a>
## type [EncodeRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/encode_decode.go#L18>)

EncodeRequestFunc encodes the passed request object into the HTTP request object. It's designed to be used in HTTP clients, for client\-side endpoints. One straightforward EncodeRequestFunc could be something that JSON encodes the object directly to the request body.

```go
type EncodeRequestFunc func(context.Context, *http.Request, interface{}) error
```

<a name="EncodeResponseFunc"></a>
## type [EncodeResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/encode_decode.go#L30>)

EncodeResponseFunc encodes the passed response object to the HTTP response writer. It's designed to be used in HTTP servers, for server\-side endpoints. One straightforward EncodeResponseFunc could be something that JSON encodes the object directly to the response body.

```go
type EncodeResponseFunc func(context.Context, http.ResponseWriter, interface{}) error
```

<a name="ErrorEncoder"></a>
## type [ErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L143>)

ErrorEncoder is responsible for encoding an error to the ResponseWriter. Users are encouraged to use custom ErrorEncoders to encode HTTP errors to their clients, and will likely want to pass and check for their own error types. See the example shipping/handling service.

```go
type ErrorEncoder func(ctx context.Context, err error, w http.ResponseWriter)
```

<a name="HTTPClient"></a>
## type [HTTPClient](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/client.go#L17-L19>)

HTTPClient is an interface that models \*http.Client.

```go
type HTTPClient interface {
    Do(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error)
}
```

<a name="Headerer"></a>
## type [Headerer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L223-L225>)

Headerer is checked by DefaultErrorEncoder. If an error value implements Headerer, the provided headers will be applied to the response writer, after the Content\-Type is set.

```go
type Headerer interface {
    Headers() http.Header
}
```

<a name="RequestFunc"></a>
## type [RequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/request_response_funcs.go#L12>)

RequestFunc may take information from an HTTP request and put it into a request context. In Servers, RequestFuncs are executed prior to invoking the endpoint. In Clients, RequestFuncs are executed after creating the request but prior to invoking the HTTP client.

```go
type RequestFunc func(context.Context, *http.Request) context.Context
```

### func [SetRequestHeader](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/request_response_funcs.go#L39>)

```go
func SetRequestHeader(key, val string) RequestFunc
```

SetRequestHeader returns a RequestFunc that sets the given header.


ServeHTTP implements http.Handler.

<a name="ServerFinalizerFunc"></a>
## type [ServerFinalizerFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L150>)

ServerFinalizerFunc can be used to perform work at the end of an HTTP request, after the response has been written to the client. The principal intended use is for request logging. In addition to the response code provided in the function signature, additional response parameters are provided in the context under keys with the ContextKeyResponse prefix.

ServerOption sets an optional parameter for servers.


```go
func ServerAfter(after ...ServerResponseFunc) ServerOption
```

ServerAfter functions are executed on the HTTP response writer after the endpoint is invoked, but before anything is written to the client.

### func [ServerBefore](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L51>)

```go

```

<a name="ServerErrorEncoder"></a>
### func [ServerErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L65>)

```go
func ServerErrorEncoder(ee ErrorEncoder) ServerOption
```

ServerErrorEncoder is used to encode errors to the http.ResponseWriter whenever they're encountered in the processing of a request. Clients can use this to provide custom error formatting and response codes. By default, errors will be written with the DefaultErrorEncoder.

<a name="ServerErrorHandler"></a>
### func [ServerErrorHandler](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L84>)

```go
func ServerErrorHandler(errorHandler transport.ErrorHandler) ServerOption
```

ServerErrorHandler is used to handle non\-terminal errors. By default, non\-terminal errors are ignored. This is intended as a diagnostic measure. Finer\-grained control of error handling, including logging in more detail, should be performed in a custom ServerErrorEncoder or ServerFinalizer, both of which have access to the context.

<a name="ServerErrorLogger"></a>
### func [ServerErrorLogger](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L75>)

```go
func ServerErrorLogger(logger log.Logger) ServerOption
```

ServerErrorLogger is used to log non\-terminal errors. By default, no errors are logged. This is intended as a diagnostic measure. Finer\-grained control of error handling, including logging in more detail, should be performed in a custom ServerErrorEncoder or ServerFinalizer, both of which have access to the context. Deprecated: Use ServerErrorHandler instead.

<a name="ServerFinalizer"></a>
### func [ServerFinalizer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L90>)

```go
func ServerFinalizer(f ...ServerFinalizerFunc) ServerOption
```

ServerFinalizer is executed at the end of every HTTP request. By default, no finalizer is registered.

<a name="ServerResponseFunc"></a>
## type [ServerResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/request_response_funcs.go#L17>)

ServerResponseFunc may take information from a request context and use it to manipulate a ResponseWriter. ServerResponseFuncs are only executed in servers, after invoking the endpoint but prior to writing a response.

```go
type ServerResponseFunc func(context.Context, http.ResponseWriter) context.Context
```


<a name="StatusCoder"></a>
## type [StatusCoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/server.go#L216-L218>)

StatusCoder is checked by DefaultErrorEncoder. If an error value implements StatusCoder, the StatusCode will be used when encoding the error. By default, StatusInternalServerError \(500\) is used.

```go

    StatusCode() int
}
```

# httprp

```go
import "github.com/go-kit/kit/transport/httprp"
```

Package httprp provides an HTTP reverse\-proxy transport. HTTP handlers that need to proxy requests to another HTTP service can do so with this package by specifying the URL to forward the request to.

```go

```

NewServer constructs a new server that implements http.Server and will proxy requests to the given base URL using its scheme, host, and base path. If the target's path is "/base" and the incoming request was for "/dir", the target request will be for /base/dir.

```go
func DefaultErrorEncoder(_ context.Context, err error, reply string, nc *nats.Conn)
```

DefaultErrorEncoder writes the error to the subscriber reply.

<a name="EncodeJSONRequest"></a>
## func [EncodeJSONRequest](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/publisher.go#L101>)

```go
func EncodeJSONRequest(_ context.Context, msg *nats.Msg, request interface{}) error
```

EncodeJSONRequest is an EncodeRequestFunc that serializes the request as a JSON object to the Data of the Msg. Many JSON\-over\-NATS services can use it as a sensible default.

<a name="EncodeJSONResponse"></a>
## func [EncodeJSONResponse](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L167>)

```go
func EncodeJSONResponse(_ context.Context, reply string, nc *nats.Conn, response interface{}) error
```

EncodeJSONResponse is a EncodeResponseFunc that serializes the response as a JSON object to the subscriber reply. Many JSON\-over services can use it as a sensible default.

<a name="NopRequestDecoder"></a>
## func [NopRequestDecoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L160>)

```go
func NopRequestDecoder(_ context.Context, _ *nats.Msg) (interface{}, error)
```

NopRequestDecoder is a DecodeRequestFunc that can be used for requests that do not need to be decoded, and simply returns nil, nil.

<a name="DecodeRequestFunc"></a>
## type [DecodeRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/encode_decode.go#L13>)

DecodeRequestFunc extracts a user\-domain request object from a publisher request object. It's designed to be used in NATS subscribers, for subscriber\-side endpoints. One straightforward DecodeRequestFunc could be something that JSON decodes from the request body to the concrete response type.

```go
type DecodeRequestFunc func(context.Context, *nats.Msg) (request interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="DecodeResponseFunc"></a>
## type [DecodeResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/encode_decode.go#L31>)

DecodeResponseFunc extracts a user\-domain response object from an NATS response object. It's designed to be used in NATS publisher, for publisher\-side endpoints. One straightforward DecodeResponseFunc could be something that JSON decodes from the response payload to the concrete response type.

```go
type DecodeResponseFunc func(context.Context, *nats.Msg) (response interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="EncodeRequestFunc"></a>
## type [EncodeRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/encode_decode.go#L19>)

EncodeRequestFunc encodes the passed request object into the NATS request object. It's designed to be used in NATS publishers, for publisher\-side endpoints. One straightforward EncodeRequestFunc could something that JSON encodes the object directly to the request payload.

```go
type EncodeRequestFunc func(context.Context, *nats.Msg, interface{}) error
```

<a name="EncodeResponseFunc"></a>
## type [EncodeResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/encode_decode.go#L25>)

EncodeResponseFunc encodes the passed response object to the subscriber reply. It's designed to be used in NATS subscribers, for subscriber\-side endpoints. One straightforward EncodeResponseFunc could be something that JSON encodes the object directly to the response body.

```go
type EncodeResponseFunc func(context.Context, string, *nats.Conn, interface{}) error
```

<a name="ErrorEncoder"></a>
## type [ErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L151>)

ErrorEncoder is responsible for encoding an error to the subscriber reply. Users are encouraged to use custom ErrorEncoders to encode errors to their replies, and will likely want to pass and check for their own error types.

```go
type ErrorEncoder func(ctx context.Context, err error, reply string, nc *nats.Conn)
```

<a name="PublisherResponseFunc"></a>
## type [PublisherResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/request_response_funcs.go#L22>)

PublisherResponseFunc may take information from an NATS request and make the response available for consumption. ClientResponseFuncs are only executed in clients, after a request has been made, but prior to it being decoded.

```go
type PublisherResponseFunc func(context.Context, *nats.Msg) context.Context
```

<a name="RequestFunc"></a>
## type [RequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/request_response_funcs.go#L12>)

RequestFunc may take information from a publisher request and put it into a request context. In Subscribers, RequestFuncs are executed prior to invoking the endpoint.

```go
type RequestFunc func(context.Context, *nats.Msg) context.Context
```


<a name="SubscriberFinalizerFunc"></a>
## type [SubscriberFinalizerFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L156>)

SubscriberFinalizerFunc can be used to perform work at the end of an request from a publisher, after the response has been written to the publisher. The principal intended use is for request logging.

```go
type SubscriberFinalizerFunc func(ctx context.Context, msg *nats.Msg)
```

<a name="SubscriberOption"></a>
## type [SubscriberOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L48>)

SubscriberOption sets an optional parameter for subscribers.

```go
type SubscriberOption func(*Subscriber)
```

<a name="SubscriberAfter"></a>
### func [SubscriberAfter](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L58>)

```go
func SubscriberAfter(after ...SubscriberResponseFunc) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberAfter functions are executed on the subscriber reply after the endpoint is invoked, but before anything is published to the reply.

<a name="SubscriberBefore"></a>
### func [SubscriberBefore](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L52>)

```go
func SubscriberBefore(before ...RequestFunc) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberBefore functions are executed on the publisher request object before the request is decoded.

<a name="SubscriberErrorEncoder"></a>
### func [SubscriberErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L66>)

```go
func SubscriberErrorEncoder(ee ErrorEncoder) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberErrorEncoder is used to encode errors to the subscriber reply whenever they're encountered in the processing of a request. Clients can use this to provide custom error formatting. By default, errors will be published with the DefaultErrorEncoder.

<a name="SubscriberErrorHandler"></a>
### func [SubscriberErrorHandler](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L83>)

```go
func SubscriberErrorHandler(errorHandler transport.ErrorHandler) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberErrorHandler is used to handle non\-terminal errors. By default, non\-terminal errors are ignored. This is intended as a diagnostic measure. Finer\-grained control of error handling, including logging in more detail, should be performed in a custom SubscriberErrorEncoder which has access to the context.

<a name="SubscriberErrorLogger"></a>
### func [SubscriberErrorLogger](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L75>)

```go
func SubscriberErrorLogger(logger log.Logger) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberErrorLogger is used to log non\-terminal errors. By default, no errors are logged. This is intended as a diagnostic measure. Finer\-grained control of error handling, including logging in more detail, should be performed in a custom SubscriberErrorEncoder which has access to the context. Deprecated: Use SubscriberErrorHandler instead.

<a name="SubscriberFinalizer"></a>
### func [SubscriberFinalizer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/subscriber.go#L89>)

```go
func SubscriberFinalizer(f ...SubscriberFinalizerFunc) SubscriberOption
```

SubscriberFinalizer is executed at the end of every request from a publisher through NATS. By default, no finalizer is registered.

<a name="SubscriberResponseFunc"></a>
## type [SubscriberResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/nats/request_response_funcs.go#L17>)

SubscriberResponseFunc may take information from a request context and use it to manipulate a Publisher. SubscriberResponseFuncs are only executed in subscribers, after invoking the endpoint but prior to publishing a reply.

```go
type SubscriberResponseFunc func(context.Context, *nats.Conn) context.Context
```
