## Variables

<a name="ErrConnectionUnavailable"></a>ErrConnectionUnavailable is returned by the Manager's Write method when the manager cannot yield a good connection.

```go
var ErrConnectionUnavailable = errors.New("connection unavailable")
```

<a name="Exponential"></a>
## func [Exponential](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/util/conn/manager.go#L140>)

```go
func Exponential(d time.Duration) time.Duration
```

Exponential takes a duration and returns another one that is twice as long, \+/\- 50%. It is used to provide backoff for operations that may fail and should avoid thundering herds. See https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/architecture/exponential-backoff-and-jitter/ for rationale

<a name="AfterFunc"></a>
## type [AfterFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/util/conn/manager.go#L17>)

AfterFunc imitates time.After.

```go
type AfterFunc func(time.Duration) <-chan time.Time
```

<a name="Dialer"></a>
## type [Dialer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/util/conn/manager.go#L14>)

Dialer imitates net.Dial. Dialer is assumed to yield connections that are safe for use by multiple concurrent goroutines.

```go
type Dialer func(network, address string) (net.Conn, error)
```

<a name="Manager"></a>
## type [Manager](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/util/conn/manager.go#L26-L35>)

Manager manages a net.Conn.

Clients provide a way to create the connection with a Dialer, network, and address. Clients should Take the connection when they want to use it, and Put back whatever error they receive from its use. When a non\-nil error is Put, the connection is invalidated, and a new connection is established. Connection failures are retried after an exponential backoff.

```go
type Manager struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewDefaultManager"></a>
### func [NewDefaultManager](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/util/conn/manager.go#L58>)

```go
func NewDefaultManager(network, address string, logger log.Logger) *Manager
```

NewDefaultManager is a helper constructor, suitable for most normal use in real \(non\-test\) code. It uses the real net.Dial and time.After functions.

<a name="NewManager"></a>
### func [NewManager](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/util/conn/manager.go#L41>)

```go
func NewManager(d Dialer, network, address string, after AfterFunc, logger log.Logger) *Manager
```

NewManager returns a connection manager using the passed Dialer, network, and address. The AfterFunc is used to control exponential backoff and retries. The logger is used to log errors; pass a log.NopLogger if you don't care to receive them. For normal use, prefer NewDefaultManager.

<a name="Manager.Put"></a>
### func \(\*Manager\) [Put](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/util/conn/manager.go#L70>)

```go
func (m *Manager) Put(err error)
```

Put accepts an error that came from a previously yielded connection. If the error is non\-nil, the manager will invalidate the current connection and try to reconnect, with exponential backoff. Putting a nil error is a no\-op.

<a name="Manager.Take"></a>
### func \(\*Manager\) [Take](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/util/conn/manager.go#L63>)

```go
func (m *Manager) Take() net.Conn
```

Take yields the current connection. It may be nil.

<a name="Manager.Write"></a>
### func \(\*Manager\) [Write](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/util/conn/manager.go#L75>)

```go
func (m *Manager) Write(b []byte) (int, error)
```

Write writes the passed data to the connection in a single Take/Put cycle.

<a name="LabelValues"></a>
## type [LabelValues](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/metrics/internal/lv/labelvalues.go#L6>)

LabelValues is a type alias that provides validation on its With method. Metrics may include it as a member to help them satisfy With semantics and save some code duplication.

```go
type LabelValues []string
```

<a name="LabelValues.With"></a>
### func \(LabelValues\) [With](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/metrics/internal/lv/labelvalues.go#L9>)

```go
func (lvs LabelValues) With(labelValues ...string) LabelValues
```

With validates the input, and returns a new aggregate labelValues.

<a name="Space"></a>
## type [Space](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/metrics/internal/lv/space.go#L13-L16>)

Space represents an N\-dimensional vector space. Each name and unique label value pair establishes a new dimension and point within that dimension. Order matters, i.e. \[a=1 b=2\] identifies a different timeseries than \[b=2 a=1\].

```go
type Space struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewSpace"></a>
### func [NewSpace](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/metrics/internal/lv/space.go#L6>)

```go
func NewSpace() *Space
```

NewSpace returns an N\-dimensional vector space.

<a name="Space.Add"></a>
### func \(\*Space\) [Add](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/metrics/internal/lv/space.go#L27>)

```go
func (s *Space) Add(name string, lvs LabelValues, delta float64)
```

Add locates the time series identified by the name and label values in the vector space, and appends the delta to the last value in the list of observations.

<a name="Space.Observe"></a>
### func \(\*Space\) [Observe](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/metrics/internal/lv/space.go#L20>)

```go
func (s *Space) Observe(name string, lvs LabelValues, value float64)
```

Observe locates the time series identified by the name and label values in the vector space, and appends the value to the list of observations.

<a name="Space.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*Space\) [Reset](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/metrics/internal/lv/space.go#L46>)

```go
func (s *Space) Reset() *Space
```

Reset empties the current space and returns a new Space with the old contents. Reset a Space to get an immutable copy suitable for walking.

<a name="Space.Walk"></a>
### func \(\*Space\) [Walk](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/metrics/internal/lv/space.go#L33>)

```go
func (s *Space) Walk(fn func(name string, lvs LabelValues, observations []float64) bool)
```

Walk traverses the vector space and invokes fn for each non\-empty time series which is encountered. Return false to abort the traversal.

# ratemap

```go
import "github.com/go-kit/kit/metrics/internal/ratemap"
```

Package ratemap implements a goroutine\-safe map of string to float64. It can be embedded in implementations whose metrics support fixed sample rates, so that an additional parameter doesn't have to be tracked through the e.g. lv.Space object.

# jsonrpc

```go
import "github.com/go-kit/kit/transport/http/jsonrpc"
```

Package jsonrpc provides a JSON RPC \(v2.0\) binding for endpoints. See http://www.jsonrpc.org/specification

## Constants

<a name="ParseError"></a>

```go
const (
    // ParseError defines invalid JSON was received by the server.
    // An error occurred on the server while parsing the JSON text.
    ParseError int = -32700

    // InvalidRequestError defines the JSON sent is not a valid Request object.
    InvalidRequestError int = -32600

    // MethodNotFoundError defines the method does not exist / is not available.
    MethodNotFoundError int = -32601

    // InvalidParamsError defines invalid method parameter(s).
    InvalidParamsError int = -32602

    // InternalError defines a server error
    InternalError int = -32603
)
```

<a name="Version"></a>

```go
const (
    // Version defines the version of the JSON RPC implementation
    Version string = "2.0"

    // ContentType defines the content type to be served.
    ContentType string = "application/json; charset=utf-8"
)
```

<a name="DefaultErrorEncoder"></a>
## func [DefaultErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L183>)

```go
func DefaultErrorEncoder(ctx context.Context, err error, w http.ResponseWriter)
```

DefaultErrorEncoder writes the error to the ResponseWriter, as a json\-rpc error response, with an InternalError status code. The Error\(\) string of the error will be used as the response error message. If the error implements ErrorCoder, the provided code will be set on the response error. If the error implements Headerer, the given headers will be set.

## func [DefaultRequestEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/client.go#L66>)

```go
func DefaultRequestEncoder(_ context.Context, req interface{}) (json.RawMessage, error)
```

DefaultRequestEncoder marshals the given request to JSON.


DefaultResponseDecoder unmarshals the result to interface\{\}, or returns an error, if found.

ErrorMessage returns a message for the JSON RPC error code. It returns the empty string if the code is unknown.

<a name="ClientFinalizerFunc"></a>
## type [ClientFinalizerFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/client.go#L227>)

ClientFinalizerFunc can be used to perform work at the end of a client HTTP request, after the response is returned. The principal intended use is for error logging. Additional response parameters are provided in the context under keys with the ContextKeyResponse prefix. Note: err may be nil. There maybe also no additional response parameters depending on when an error occurs.

```go
type ClientFinalizerFunc func(ctx context.Context, err error)
```

<a name="ClientOption"></a>
## type [ClientOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/client.go#L85>)

ClientOption sets an optional parameter for clients.

```go
type ClientOption func(*Client)
```

<a name="BufferedStream"></a>
### func [BufferedStream](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/client.go#L138>)

```go
func BufferedStream(buffered bool) ClientOption
```

BufferedStream sets whether the Response.Body is left open, allowing it to be read from later. Useful for transporting a file as a buffered stream.

<a name="ClientAfter"></a>
### func [ClientAfter](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/client.go#L102>)

```go
func ClientAfter(after ...httptransport.ClientResponseFunc) ClientOption
```

ClientAfter sets the ClientResponseFuncs applied to the server's HTTP response prior to it being decoded. This is useful for obtaining anything from the response and adding onto the context prior to decoding.

```go
func ClientBefore(before ...httptransport.RequestFunc) ClientOption
```

ClientBefore sets the RequestFuncs that are applied to the outgoing HTTP request before it's invoked.


ClientFinalizer is executed at the end of every HTTP request. By default, no finalizer is registered.

```go
func SetClient(client httptransport.HTTPClient) ClientOption
```

SetClient sets the underlying HTTP client used for requests. By default, http.DefaultClient is used.

<a name="DecodeRequestFunc"></a>
## type [DecodeRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/encode_decode.go#L27>)

DecodeRequestFunc extracts a user\-domain request object from raw JSON It's designed to be used in JSON RPC servers, for server\-side endpoints. One straightforward DecodeRequestFunc could be something that unmarshals JSON from the request body to the concrete request type.

```go
type DecodeRequestFunc func(context.Context, json.RawMessage) (request interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="DecodeResponseFunc"></a>
## type [DecodeResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/encode_decode.go#L48>)

DecodeResponseFunc extracts a user\-domain response object from an JSON RPC response object. It's designed to be used in JSON RPC clients, for client\-side endpoints. It is the responsibility of this function to decide whether any error present in the JSON RPC response should be surfaced to the client endpoint.

```go
type DecodeResponseFunc func(context.Context, Response) (response interface{}, err error)
```

<a name="EncodeRequestFunc"></a>
## type [EncodeRequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/encode_decode.go#L41>)

EncodeRequestFunc encodes the given request object to raw JSON. It's designed to be used in JSON RPC clients, for client\-side endpoints. One straightforward EncodeResponseFunc could be something that JSON encodes the object directly.

```go
type EncodeRequestFunc func(context.Context, interface{}) (request json.RawMessage, err error)
```

<a name="EncodeResponseFunc"></a>
## type [EncodeResponseFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/encode_decode.go#L33>)

EncodeResponseFunc encodes the passed response object to a JSON RPC result. It's designed to be used in HTTP servers, for server\-side endpoints. One straightforward EncodeResponseFunc could be something that JSON encodes the object directly.

```go
type EncodeResponseFunc func(context.Context, interface{}) (response json.RawMessage, err error)
```

<a name="EndpointCodec"></a>
## type [EndpointCodec](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/encode_decode.go#L14-L18>)

EndpointCodec defines a server Endpoint and its associated codecs

```go
type EndpointCodec struct {
    Endpoint endpoint.Endpoint
    Decode   DecodeRequestFunc
    Encode   EncodeResponseFunc
}
```

<a name="EndpointCodecMap"></a>
## type [EndpointCodecMap](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/encode_decode.go#L21>)

EndpointCodecMap maps the Request.Method to the proper EndpointCodec

```go
type EndpointCodecMap map[string]EndpointCodec
```

<a name="Error"></a>
## type [Error](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/error.go#L6-L10>)

Error defines a JSON RPC error that can be returned in a Response from the spec http://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#error_object

```go
type Error struct {
    Code    int         `json:"code"`
    Message string      `json:"message"`
    Data    interface{} `json:"data,omitempty"`
}
```

<a name="Error.Error"></a>
### func \(Error\) [Error](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/error.go#L13>)

```go
func (e Error) Error() string
```

Error implements error.

<a name="Error.ErrorCode"></a>
### func \(Error\) [ErrorCode](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/error.go#L21>)

```go
func (e Error) ErrorCode() int
```

ErrorCode returns the JSON RPC error code associated with the error.

<a name="ErrorCoder"></a>
## type [ErrorCoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L217-L219>)

ErrorCoder is checked by DefaultErrorEncoder. If an error value implements ErrorCoder, the integer result of ErrorCode\(\) will be used as the JSONRPC error code when encoding the error.

By default, InternalError \(\-32603\) is used.

```go
type ErrorCoder interface {
    ErrorCode() int
}
```

<a name="Request"></a>
## type [Request](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/request_response_types.go#L11-L16>)

Request defines a JSON RPC request from the spec http://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#request_object

```go
type Request struct {
    JSONRPC string          `json:"jsonrpc"`
    Method  string          `json:"method"`
    Params  json.RawMessage `json:"params"`
    ID      *RequestID      `json:"id"`
}
```

<a name="RequestFunc"></a>
## type [RequestFunc](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/request_response_types.go#L36>)

RequestFunc may take information from decoded json body and place in request context. In Servers, RequestFuncs are executed after json is parsed but prior to invoking the codec

```go
type RequestFunc func(context.Context, *http.Request, Request) context.Context
```

<a name="RequestID"></a>
## type [RequestID](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/request_response_types.go#L24-L31>)

RequestID defines a request ID that can be string, number, or null. An identifier established by the Client that MUST contain a String, Number, or NULL value if included. If it is not included it is assumed to be a notification. The value SHOULD normally not be Null and Numbers SHOULD NOT contain fractional parts.

```go
type RequestID struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="RequestID.Float32"></a>
### func \(\*RequestID\) [Float32](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/request_response_types.go#L65>)

```go
func (id *RequestID) Float32() (float32, error)
```

Float32 returns the ID as a float value. An error is returned if the ID can't be treated as an float.

<a name="RequestID.Int"></a>
### func \(\*RequestID\) [Int](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/request_response_types.go#L59>)

```go
func (id *RequestID) Int() (int, error)
```

Int returns the ID as an integer value. An error is returned if the ID can't be treated as an int.


```go
func (id *RequestID) String() (string, error)
```

String returns the ID as a string value. An error is returned if the ID can't be treated as an string.

<a name="Response"></a>
## type [Response](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/request_response_types.go#L77-L82>)

Response defines a JSON RPC response from the spec http://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#response_object

```go
    JSONRPC string          `json:"jsonrpc"`
```

## type [Server](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L19-L27>)

Server wraps an endpoint and implements http.Handler.

```go
type Server struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewServer"></a>
### func [NewServer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L30-L33>)

```go
func NewServer(ecm EndpointCodecMap, options ...ServerOption) *Server
```

NewServer constructs a new server, which implements http.Server.

<a name="Server.ServeHTTP"></a>
### func \(Server\) [ServeHTTP](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L92>)

```go
func (s Server) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
```

ServeHTTP implements http.Handler.

<a name="ServerOption"></a>
## type [ServerOption](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L46>)

ServerOption sets an optional parameter for servers.

```go
type ServerOption func(*Server)
```

<a name="ServerAfter"></a>
### func [ServerAfter](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L64>)

```go
func ServerAfter(after ...httptransport.ServerResponseFunc) ServerOption
```

ServerAfter functions are executed on the HTTP response writer after the endpoint is invoked, but before anything is written to the client.

<a name="ServerBefore"></a>
### func [ServerBefore](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L50>)

```go
func ServerBefore(before ...httptransport.RequestFunc) ServerOption
```

ServerBefore functions are executed on the HTTP request object before the request is decoded.

<a name="ServerBeforeCodec"></a>
### func [ServerBeforeCodec](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L58>)

```go
func ServerBeforeCodec(beforeCodec ...RequestFunc) ServerOption
```

ServerBeforeCodec functions are executed after the JSON request body has been decoded, but before the method's decoder is called. This provides an opportunity for middleware to inspect the contents of the rpc request before being passed to the codec.

<a name="ServerErrorEncoder"></a>
### func [ServerErrorEncoder](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L72>)

```go
func ServerErrorEncoder(ee httptransport.ErrorEncoder) ServerOption
```

ServerErrorEncoder is used to encode errors to the http.ResponseWriter whenever they're encountered in the processing of a request. Clients can use this to provide custom error formatting and response codes. By default, errors will be written with the DefaultErrorEncoder.

<a name="ServerErrorLogger"></a>
### func [ServerErrorLogger](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L81>)

```go
func ServerErrorLogger(logger log.Logger) ServerOption
```

ServerErrorLogger is used to log non\-terminal errors. By default, no errors are logged. This is intended as a diagnostic measure. Finer\-grained control of error handling, including logging in more detail, should be performed in a custom ServerErrorEncoder or ServerFinalizer, both of which have access to the context.

<a name="ServerFinalizer"></a>
### func [ServerFinalizer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/jsonrpc/server.go#L87>)

```go
func ServerFinalizer(f httptransport.ServerFinalizerFunc) ServerOption
```

ServerFinalizer is executed at the end of every HTTP request. By default, no finalizer is registered.


<a name="EncodeProtoRequest"></a>
## func [EncodeProtoRequest](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/proto/client.go#L18>)

```go
func EncodeProtoRequest(_ context.Context, r *http.Request, preq interface{}) error
```

EncodeProtoRequest is an EncodeRequestFunc that serializes the request as Protobuf. If the request implements Headerer, the provided headers will be applied to the request. If the given request does not implement proto.Message, an error will be returned.

<a name="EncodeProtoResponse"></a>
## func [EncodeProtoResponse](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/http/proto/server.go#L16>)

```go
func EncodeProtoResponse(ctx context.Context, w http.ResponseWriter, pres interface{}) error
```

EncodeProtoResponse is an EncodeResponseFunc that serializes the response as Protobuf. Many Proto\-over\-HTTP services can use it as a sensible default. If the response implements Headerer, the provided headers will be applied to the response. If the response implements StatusCoder, the provided StatusCode will be used instead of 200.

<a name="Test_ServiceDesc"></a>Test\_ServiceDesc is the grpc.ServiceDesc for Test service. It's only intended for direct use with grpc.RegisterService, and not to be introspected or modified \(even as a copy\)

```go
var Test_ServiceDesc = grpc.ServiceDesc{
    ServiceName: "pb.Test",
    HandlerType: (*TestServer)(nil),
    Methods: []grpc.MethodDesc{
        {
            MethodName: "Test",
            Handler:    _Test_Test_Handler,
        },
    },
    Streams:  []grpc.StreamDesc{},
    Metadata: "test.proto",
}
```

<a name="TestClient"></a>
## type [TestClient](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/_grpc_test/pb/test_grpc.pb.go#L20-L22>)

TestClient is the client API for Test service.

For semantics around ctx use and closing/ending streaming RPCs, please refer to https://pkg.go.dev/google.golang.org/grpc/?tab=doc#ClientConn.NewStream.

```go
type TestClient interface {
    Test(ctx context.Context, in *TestRequest, opts ...grpc.CallOption) (*TestResponse, error)
}
```

```go
}
```

## type [UnsafeTestServer](<https://github.com/go-kit/kit/blob/master/transport/grpc/_grpc_test/pb/test_grpc.pb.go#L61-L63>)

UnsafeTestServer may be embedded to opt out of forward compatibility for this service. Use of this interface is not recommended, as added methods to TestServer will result in compilation errors.
